question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage transaction with a crafted receipt containing an arbitrary topicRunningHash byte sequence, causing the mirror node to persist a falsified running hash in the topic_message table and permanently corrupt the verifiable hash chain for that topic?
